que: What is the primary purpose of the Theme widget in Flutter?
options: To apply a ThemeData to descendant widgets for consistent visual styling

que: What type of object does the Theme widget apply to its descendants?
What happens to widgets below a Theme widget in the widget tree?
options: They inherit the ThemeData defined by the Theme
They must define their own ThemeData
They can no longer be styled

que: Which of the following is a common use case of the Theme widget?
options: Applying a consistent theme across your app

que: Which of these is mentioned as a use case for Theme?
options: Implementing dark mode or light mode

In the example, which brightness is used in ThemeData?
In the example, which TextTheme property is customized?
In the example, what text is shown in the AppBar title?
In the example, what text is displayed in the body?
According to the class definition, what does Theme extend?
Which parameter of Theme holds the ThemeData to apply?
Which parameter of Theme indicates whether this is the MaterialApp’s theme?
Which parameter of Theme represents the widget below it in the tree?
What is the type of the data property in the Theme class?
What is the type of the isMaterialAppTheme property in Theme?
Which of the following is listed as a way to override the default Material theme?
options: Wrap parts of the UI in a Theme widget with custom ThemeData

Which of these is a good use case for wrapping a subtree with a Theme widget?
options: To apply a special color scheme to just one section of the app
